summary: Tested 5 new features (Forgot Password, WhatsApp Bot, Multi-image Products, Edit Product, Share Pack) on Lapakin. Backend: 45/45 pytest tests pass (existing 19 + 26 new for forgot/reset, multi-image, WhatsApp webhook & parser). Frontend: full E2E flows validated end-to-end including forgot→reset→login with new password, dashboard nav with WhatsApp item, /dashboard/whatsapp page (twilio-not-configured banner, linked state, pair code generation), Edit Product dialog (all fields + 3 image thumbnails, save updates list), AI Studio multi-image upload (2 thumbnails), and Storefront lightbox with prev/next + 1/3 counter.

minor.issue: Email enumeration via timing/payload size: known emails return JSON with 'reset_token','expires_in_minutes','simple_mode' while unknown emails return only 'ok'+'message'. While simple-mode is intentional for MVP, the differing payload structure trivially leaks whether an email is registered. Acceptable for MVP per spec but should be flagged before production switch to real email-send.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew from ~540 to ~826 lines covering 5 resource groups (auth, shops, products, AI, WhatsApp) plus forgot-password. Splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/products.py, routers/whatsapp.py, services/twilio.py is overdue — file is hard to navigate and review.

/api/auth/reset-password — the 'auth_provider' computation does an extra DB read inside $set. The ternary `'both' if (await db.users.find_one(...))` ... `else 'email'` reads the user inline; cleaner to fetch once before update_one. Also: forgot-password creates a NEW token doc on every request — no rate limiting and old tokens stay valid until TTL expires (1h). Consider invalidating prior unused tokens for the same user.
Forgot-password leaks registration status via differing payload shape (see backend_issues.minor). Should make response identical for known/unknown emails when graduating from simple_mode.

WhatsApp webhook has NO Twilio signature verification. Anyone can POST to /api/whatsapp/webhook and trigger pairing/list/unlink. Acceptable for sandbox but MUST be added before production (X-Twilio-Signature HMAC).

_parse_product_text handles common formats well, but when stock is mentioned BEFORE price (e.g. 'Kopi stok 20 25000') the stock parser strips everything after stock and price detection gets the wrong substring. Documented assumption: stock keyword always trails price.

/api/whatsapp/connect/start uses upsert keyed by user_id which is correct, but the index `wa_pair_codes.user_id unique=True` means generating a new code overwrites the previous one — fine. However wa_pair_codes.code is NOT unique, so if two users happen to get the same 6-digit code (1 in 900k chance) the webhook lookup picks an arbitrary one. Consider unique on code with retry, or use 8 digits.
Frontend EditProductDialog: cancel button correctly uses data-testid='edit-cancel-btn'; all input testids consistent. No issues found.
Storefront lightbox does not have role='dialog' or aria-modal; minor a11y gap (still functional with keyboard? unclear). Consider adding role='dialog' aria-modal='true' for screen readers.
AI Studio file-input has multiple=true (verified). Good.

Twilio outbound send (_wa_send) is no-op when env not set — correct degradation. But _download_media also requires Twilio creds so media-based product creation cannot be exercised end-to-end without env vars.
test_credentials: sari@warung.id password was reset to 'newpass123' during this run (was 'sari1234'). Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md if persisted. admin@lapakin.id / lapakin123 still works.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 test product 'Kopi Test Updated' (price 30000) on warung-sari shop with 3 SVG inline images via direct API call. Multiple TEST_<runid>@example.com pytest users left in DB (no cleanup hook). WhatsApp link for sari was created+disconnected during webhook tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v (45 tests, ~31s incl. 3 AI calls). New testids verified working: goto-forgot-link, forgot-{email-input,submit-btn}, reset-link-card, open-reset-page-btn, copy-reset-link-btn, reset-{token,password,confirm}-input, reset-submit-btn, nav-whatsapp, twilio-not-configured, wa-{linked-state,disconnect-btn,pair-code,generate-code-btn,check-status-btn}, edit-{product_id} as button id pattern (NOT prefix), edit-product-dialog, edit-{name,price,stock,description,ig,tiktok,hashtags,add-image,cancel-btn,save-btn}-input/btn, edit-img-{i}, thumb-{i} on AI Studio. Storefront lightbox opens on image click (no role=dialog though). Sari password is now 'newpass123'. Twilio env intentionally unset.
rca of the issue: No bugs found requiring fix. Two MVP-acceptable concerns documented for production hardening: (1) forgot-password response shape leaks email enumeration; (2) /api/whatsapp/webhook lacks Twilio HMAC signature verification.
